name: Spartan Chess
author: Steven Streetman (2010); platform port AGP
description: Spartan Chess (Steven Streetman, 2010): an asymmetric-army variant. The Persians (White) field an orthodox FIDE army with one King; the Spartans (Black) field two Kings and four unique pieces -- the General (rook + one diagonal step), Warlord (bishop + knight), Captain (one or two squares orthogonally, jumping), Lieutenant (one or two squares diagonally jumping, plus a non-capturing sideways step) and the Hoplite, a pawn that steps diagonally forward and captures straight ahead. While the Spartan has two Kings they are immune from ordinary check: only a simultaneous attack on BOTH Kings (duple-check) is binding. The Persian may capture a Spartan King as an ordinary capture; a Hoplite reaching the last rank may regain a second King if only one remains. Persians castle and may capture en passant; Spartans do neither. White moves first.
